title: Mildly Smoky Ethiopian Dates Tagine
description: A sweet and savory Ethiopian dish featuring medjool dates and a blend of herbal spices, with a hint of smokiness and no added sugar.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the berbere spice and smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the pitted medjool dates and cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the dates are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve the tagine hot over fluffy injera bread or couscous.
